affinity.description: Affinity rules for the operator pod.

enabled.description: Enable the Bitwarden SDK server sub-chart.

namespaceOverride.description: Override the namespace for the Bitwarden SDK server.

affinity.description: Affinity rules for the cert controller pod.

create.description: Specifies whether a certificate controller deployment be created.

extraArgs.description: Map of extra arguments to pass to container.

extraEnv.description: Extra environment variables to add to container.

extraInitContainers.description: Extra init containers to add to the pod.

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volumes to mount to the container.

extraVolumes.description: Extra volumes to pass to pod.

hostNetwork.description: Run the certController on the host network

image.description: Container image configuration for the cert controller.

flavour.description: Image flavor for the cert controller.

pullPolicy.description: Image pull policy for the cert controller.

repository.description: Image repository for the cert controller.

tag.description: Image tag for the cert controller.

imagePullSecrets.description: Docker registry secrets for pulling cert controller images.

log.description: Specifies Log Params to the Certificate Controller

level.description: Log level for the cert controller.

metrics.description: Metrics configuration for the cert controller.

enabled.description: Enable if you use another monitoring tool than Prometheus to scrape the metrics

nodeSelector.description: Node selector for the cert controller pod.

podDisruptionBudget.description: Pod disruption budget - for more details see https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/disruptions/

enabled.description: Enable pod disruption budget for the cert controller.

minAvailable.description: Minimum number of pods that must be available.

nameOverride.description: Override the name of the PDB resource.

podLabels.description: Extra labels to add to the cert controller pod.

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context for the cert controller.

rbac.description: RBAC configuration for the cert controller.

create.description: Specifies whether role and rolebinding resources should be created.

readinessProbe.description: Readiness probe configuration for the cert controller.

replicaCount.description: Number of replicas for the cert controller deployment.

requeueInterval.description: Requeue interval for the cert controller reconciliation loop.

resources.description: Resource requests and limits for the cert controller container.

revisionHistoryLimit.description: Specifies the amount of historic ReplicaSets k8s should keep (see https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#clean-up-policy)

serviceAccount.description: Service account configuration for the cert controller.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the service account.

automount.description: Automounts the service account token in all containers of the pod

create.description: Specifies whether a service account should be created.

extraLabels.description: Extra Labels to add to the service account.

name.description: The name of the service account to use. If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template.

startupProbe.description: Startup probe configuration for the cert controller.

enabled.description: Enabled determines if the startup probe should be used or not. By default it's enabled

useReadinessProbePort.description: whether to use the readiness probe port for startup probe.

tolerations.description: Tolerations for the cert controller pod.

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Topology spread constraints for the cert controller pod.

commonLabels.description: Additional labels added to all helm chart resources.

concurrent.description: Specifies the number of concurrent ExternalSecret Reconciles external-secret executes at a time.

controllerClass.description: If set external secrets will filter matching Secret Stores with the appropriate controller values.

enabled.description: Conversion is disabled by default as we stopped supporting v1alpha1.

createClusterExternalSecret.description: If true, create CRDs for Cluster External Secret.

createClusterGenerator.description: If true, create CRDs for Cluster Generator.

createClusterPushSecret.description: If true, create CRDs for Cluster Push Secret.

createClusterSecretStore.description: If true, create CRDs for Cluster Secret Store.

createPushSecret.description: If true, create CRDs for Push Secret.

createOperator.description: Specifies whether an external secret operator deployment be created.

enableHTTP2.description: if true, HTTP2 will be enabled for the services created by all controllers, curently metrics and webhook.

extendedMetricLabels.description: If true external secrets will use recommended kubernetes annotations as prometheus metric labels.

extraArgs.description: Map of extra arguments to pass to container.

extraContainers.description: Extra containers to add to the pod.

extraEnv.description: Extra environment variables to add to container.

extraInitContainers.description: Extra init containers to add to the pod.

extraObjects.description: Extra Kubernetes objects to deploy with the helm chart

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volumes to mount to the container.

extraVolumes.description: Extra volumes to pass to pod.

fullnameOverride.description: Override the full name of the chart.

crds.description: CRD installation configuration for Giant Swarm.

install.description: Whether to install CRDs via a pre-install job.

podName.description: Name of the pod used for CRD installation.

resources.description: Resource requests and limits for the CRD installation job.

image.description: Image repository for the cert controller.

tag.description: Image tag override; defaults to the chart appVersion.

image.description: Image repository for the external-secrets operator.

tag.description: Image tag override; defaults to the chart appVersion.

pullPolicy.description: Image pull policy for all containers.

tag.description: Image tag override; defaults to the chart appVersion.

resources.description: Resource requests and limits for Giant Swarm managed containers.

certController.description: Resource requests and limits for the cert controller.

externalSecrets.description: Resource requests and limits for the external-secrets operator.

webhook.description: Resource requests and limits for the webhook.

securityContext.description: Security context overrides for Giant Swarm managed containers.

certController.description: Security context for the cert controller container.

externalSecrets.description: Security context for the external-secrets operator container.

webhook.description: Security context for the webhook container.

verticalPodAutoscaler.description: Vertical Pod Autoscaler configuration. Allows VPA to be used if it's installed in the cluster.

force.description: Enables bypassing "autoscaling.k8s.io/v1" check; use with care.

global.description: Global values shared across all sub-charts.

adaptSecurityContext.description: Manages the securityContext properties to make them compatible with OpenShift. Possible values: auto - Apply configurations if it is detected that OpenShift is the target platform. force - Always apply configurations. disabled - No modification applied.

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Topology spread constraints for all pods.

annotations.description: Annotations that ConfigMaps can have to get configured in Grafana, See: sidecar.dashboards.folderAnnotation for specifying the dashboard folder. https://github.com/grafana/helm-charts/tree/main/charts/grafana

enabled.description: If true creates a Grafana dashboard.

extraLabels.description: Extra labels to add to the Grafana dashboard ConfigMap.

sidecarLabel.description: Label that ConfigMaps should have to be loaded as dashboards.

sidecarLabelValue.description: Label value that ConfigMaps should have to be loaded as dashboards.

hostNetwork.description: Run the controller on the host network

image.description: Container image configuration for the operator.

pullPolicy.description: Image pull policy for the operator.

imagePullSecrets.description: Docker registry secrets for pulling images.

leaderElect.description: If true, external-secrets will perform leader election between instances to ensure no more than one instance of external-secrets operates at a time.

livenessProbe.description: Liveness probe configuration for the operator.

enabled.description: Enabled determines if the liveness probe should be used or not. By default it's disabled.

spec.description: The body of the liveness probe settings.

failureThreshold.description: Number of consecutive probe failures that should occur before considering the probe as failed.

port.description: Set this value to 8082 to active liveness probes.

initialDelaySeconds.description: Delay in seconds for the container to start before performing the initial probe.

periodSeconds.description: Period in seconds for K8s to start performing probes.

successThreshold.description: Number of successful probes to mark probe successful.

timeoutSeconds.description: Specify the maximum amount of time to wait for a probe to respond before considering it fails.

log.description: Specifies Log Params to the External Secrets Operator

certDir.description: if those are not set or invalid, self-signed certs will be generated

enabled.description: Enable TLS for the metrics endpoint.
Enable if you use another monitoring tool than Prometheus to scrape the metrics

nameOverride.description: Override the name of the chart.

namespaceOverride.description: Override the namespace for the chart.

nodeSelector.description: Node selector for the operator pod.

openshiftFinalizers.description: If true the OpenShift finalizer permissions will be added to RBAC

podDisruptionBudget.description: Pod disruption budget - for more details see https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/disruptions/

minAvailable.description: Minimum number of pods that must be available.

nameOverride.description: Override the name of the PDB resource.

podLabels.description: Extra labels to add to Pod.

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context for the operator.

podSpecExtra.description: Additional pod spec fields to add to the operator pod.

processClusterExternalSecret.description: if true, the operator will process cluster external secret. Else, it will ignore them. When enabled, this adds update/patch permissions on namespaces to handle finalizers for proper cleanup during namespace deletion, preventing race conditions with ExternalSecrets.

processClusterGenerator.description: if true, the operator will process cluster generator. Else, it will ignore them.

processClusterPushSecret.description: if true, the operator will process cluster push secret. Else, it will ignore them.

processClusterStore.description: if true, the operator will process cluster store. Else, it will ignore them.

processPushSecret.description: if true, the operator will process push secret. Else, it will ignore them.

aggregateToEdit.description: Specifies whether permissions are aggregated to the edit ClusterRole

aggregateToView.description: Specifies whether permissions are aggregated to the view ClusterRole

create.description: Specifies whether role and rolebinding resources should be created.
Specifies whether a clusterrole to give servicebindings read access should be created.

replicaCount.description: Number of replicas for the external-secrets operator deployment.

resources.description: Resource requests and limits for the operator container.

revisionHistoryLimit.description: Specifies the amount of historic ReplicaSets k8s should keep (see https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#clean-up-policy)

scopedNamespace.description: If set external secrets are only reconciled in the provided namespace

scopedRBAC.description: Must be used with scopedNamespace. If true, create scoped RBAC roles under the scoped namespace and implicitly disable cluster stores and cluster external secrets

service.description: Kubernetes Service configuration for the operator.

ipFamilies.description: Sets the families that should be supported and the order in which they should be applied to ClusterIP as well. Can be IPv4 and/or IPv6.

ipFamilyPolicy.description: Set the ip family policy to configure dual-stack see [Configure dual-stack](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/services-networking/dual-stack/#services)

serviceAccount.description: Service account configuration for the operator.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the service account.

automount.description: Automounts the service account token in all containers of the pod

create.description: Specifies whether a service account should be created.

extraLabels.description: Extra Labels to add to the service account.

name.description: The name of the service account to use. If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template.

serviceMonitor.description: ServiceMonitor configuration for Prometheus metrics collection.

enabled.description: Specifies whether to create a ServiceMonitor resource for collecting Prometheus metrics

honorLabels.description: Let prometheus add an exported_ prefix to conflicting labels

metricRelabelings.description: Metric relabel configs to apply to samples before ingestion. [Metric Relabeling](https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#metric_relabel_configs)

namespace.description: namespace where you want to install ServiceMonitors

relabelings.description: Relabel configs to apply to samples before ingestion. [Relabeling](https://prometheus.io/docs/prometheus/latest/configuration/configuration/#relabel_config)

renderMode.description: How should we react to missing CRD "`monitoring.coreos.com/v1/ServiceMonitor`"  Possible values: - `skipIfMissing`: Only render ServiceMonitor resources if CRD is present, skip if missing. - `failIfMissing`: Fail Helm install if CRD is not present. - `alwaysRender` : Always render ServiceMonitor resources, do not check for CRD.

scrapeTimeout.description: Timeout if metrics can't be retrieved in given time interval

systemAuthDelegator.description: If true the system:auth-delegator ClusterRole will be added to RBAC

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Topology spread constraints for the operator pod.

affinity.description: Affinity rules for the webhook pod.

annotations.description: Annotations to place on validating webhook configuration.

certCheckInterval.description: Specifies the time to check if the cert is valid

certManager.description: Cert-manager integration for webhook TLS certificates.

addInjectorAnnotations.description: Automatically add the cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from annotation to the webhooks and CRDs. As long as you have the cert-manager CA Injector enabled, this will automatically setup your webhook's CA to the one used by cert-manager. See https://cert-manager.io/docs/concepts/ca-injector

annotations.description: Add extra annotations to the Certificate resource.

create.description: Create a certificate resource within this chart. See https://cert-manager.io/docs/usage/certificate/

duration.description: Set the requested duration (i.e. lifetime) of the Certificate. See https://cert-manager.io/docs/reference/api-docs/#cert-manager.io/v1.CertificateSpec One year by default.

issuerRef.description: For the Certificate created by this chart, setup the issuer. See https://cert-manager.io/docs/reference/api-docs/#cert-manager.io/v1.IssuerSpec

kind.description: Kind of the issuer (Issuer or ClusterIssuer).

name.description: Name of the issuer to use.

renewBefore.description: How long before the currently issued certificate's expiry cert-manager should renew the certificate. See https://cert-manager.io/docs/reference/api-docs/#cert-manager.io/v1.CertificateSpec Note that renewBefore should be greater than .webhook.lookaheadInterval since the webhook will check this far in advance that the certificate is valid.

revisionHistoryLimit.description: Set the revisionHistoryLimit on the Certificate. See https://cert-manager.io/docs/reference/api-docs/#cert-manager.io/v1.CertificateSpec Defaults to 0 (ignored).

enabled.description: Enabling cert-manager support will disable the built in secret and switch to using cert-manager (installed separately) to automatically issue and renew the webhook certificate. This chart does not install cert-manager for you, See https://cert-manager.io/docs/

create.description: Specifies whether a webhook deployment be created. If set to false, crds.conversion.enabled should also be set to false otherwise the kubeapi will be hammered because the conversion is looking for a webhook endpoint.

extraArgs.description: Map of extra arguments to pass to container.

extraEnv.description: Extra environment variables to add to container.

extraInitContainers.description: Extra init containers to add to the pod.

extraVolumeMounts.description: Extra volumes to mount to the container.

extraVolumes.description: Extra volumes to pass to pod.

failurePolicy.description: Specifies whether validating webhooks should be created with failurePolicy: Fail or Ignore

hostNetwork.description: Specifies if webhook pod should use hostNetwork or not.

image.description: Container image configuration for the webhook.

pullPolicy.description: Image pull policy for the webhook.

imagePullSecrets.description: Docker registry secrets for pulling webhook images.

log.description: Specifies Log Params to the Webhook

lookaheadInterval.description: Specifies the lookaheadInterval for certificate validity

enabled.description: Enable if you use another monitoring tool than Prometheus to scrape the metrics

nodeSelector.description: Node selector for the webhook pod.

podDisruptionBudget.description: Pod disruption budget - for more details see https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/pods/disruptions/

enabled.description: Enable pod disruption budget for the webhook.

minAvailable.description: Minimum number of pods that must be available.

nameOverride.description: Override the name of the PDB resource.

podLabels.description: Extra labels to add to the webhook pod.

podSecurityContext.description: Pod-level security context for the webhook.

port.description: The port the webhook will listen to

readinessProbe.description: Readiness probe configuration for the webhook.

replicaCount.description: Number of replicas for the webhook deployment.

resources.description: Resource requests and limits for the webhook container.

revisionHistoryLimit.description: Specifies the amount of historic ReplicaSets k8s should keep (see https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/workloads/controllers/deployment/#clean-up-policy)

service.description: Manage the service through which the webhook is reached.

annotations.description: Custom annotations for the webhook service.

enabled.description: Whether the service object should be enabled or not (it is expected to exist).

labels.description: Custom labels for the webhook service.

loadBalancerIP.description: If the webhook service type is LoadBalancer, you can assign a specific load balancer IP here. Check the documentation of your load balancer provider to see if/how this should be used.

type.description: The service type of the webhook service.

serviceAccount.description: Service account configuration for the webhook.

annotations.description: Annotations to add to the service account.

automount.description: Automounts the service account token in all containers of the pod

create.description: Specifies whether a service account should be created.

extraLabels.description: Extra Labels to add to the service account.

name.description: The name of the service account to use. If not set and create is true, a name is generated using the fullname template.

topologySpreadConstraints.description: Topology spread constraints for the webhook pod.
